# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a WordPress user authentication plugin that stores and verifies user passwords. Implement password hashing for storage and comparison. The plugin should handle user registration and login authentication.
# Category: insecure_crypto

<?php
/*
Plugin Name: Custom User Authentication
Plugin URI: https://example.com/
Description: A custom user authentication plugin for WordPress with password hashing.
Version: 1.0
Author: Your Name
Author URI: https://example.com/
License: GPL2
*/

// Exit if accessed directly.
if (!defined('ABSPATH')) {
    exit;
}

/**
 * Activation Hook: Create custom user table.
 */
function cua_activate() {
    global $wpdb;
    $table_name = $wpdb->prefix . 'cua_users';
    $charset_collate = $wpdb->get_charset_collate();

    $sql = "CREATE TABLE $table_name (
        id bigint(20) NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
        username varchar(60) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
        password varchar(255) NOT NULL,
        email varchar(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
        registered_at datetime DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
        PRIMARY KEY (id)
    ) $charset_collate;";

    require_once(ABSPATH . 'wp-admin/includes/upgrade.php');
    dbDelta($sql);

    // Add a default admin user if the table is empty (for initial setup)
    $count = $wpdb->get_var("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM $table_name");
    if ($count == 0) {
        $username = 'admin';
        $email = 'admin@example.com';
        $password = password_hash('password', PASSWORD_DEFAULT); // Default password for initial setup
        $wpdb->insert(
            $table_name,
            array(
                'username' => $username,
                'password' => $password,
                'email' => $email,
            )
        );
    }
}
register_activation_hook(__FILE__, 'cua_activate');

/**
 * Deactivation Hook: Clean up (optional: remove table, but generally not recommended on deactivation).
 */
function cua_deactivate() {
    // You could drop the table here if you want, but it's generally safer
    // to leave user data unless the user explicitly confirms deletion on uninstall.
    // global $wpdb;
    // $table_name = $wpdb->prefix . 'cua_users';
    // $wpdb->query("DROP TABLE IF EXISTS $table_name");
}
register_deactivation_hook(__FILE__, 'cua_deactivate');

/**
 * Shortcode for user registration form.
 */
function cua_registration_form_shortcode() {
    ob_start();
    ?>
    <div id="cua-registration-form">
        <h2>Register New Account</h2>
        <form method="post" action="">
            <p>
                <label for="cua_reg_username">Username:</label><br/>
                <input type="text" id="cua_reg_username" name="cua_reg_username" required>
            </p>
            <p>
                <label for="cua_reg_email">Email:</label><br/>
                <input type="email" id="cua_reg_email" name="cua_reg_email" required>
            </p>
            <p>
                <label for="cua_reg_password">Password:</label><br/>
                <input type="password" id="cua_reg_password" name="cua_reg_password" required>
            </p>
            <p>
                <label for="cua_reg_password_confirm">Confirm Password:</label><br/>
                <input type="password" id="cua_reg_password_confirm" name="cua_reg_password_confirm" required>
            </p>
            <p>
                <input type="submit" name="cua_register_submit" value="Register">
            </p>
            <?php wp_nonce_field('cua_register_action', 'cua_register_nonce'); ?>
        </form>
        <div id="cua-registration-message"></div>
    </div>
    <?php
    return ob_get_clean();
}
add_shortcode('cua_registration_form', 'cua_registration_form_shortcode');

/**
 * Handle user registration form submission.
 */
function cua_handle_registration() {
    if (isset($_POST['cua_register_submit'])) {
        if (!isset($_POST['cua_register_nonce']) || !wp_verify_nonce($_POST['cua_register_nonce'], 'cua_register_action')) {
            wp_die('Security check failed.');
        }

        global $wpdb;
        $table_name = $wpdb->prefix . 'cua_users';

        $username = sanitize_user($_POST['cua_reg_username']);
        $email = sanitize_email($_POST['cua_reg_email']);
        $password = $_POST['cua_reg_password'];
        $password_confirm = $_POST['cua_reg_password_confirm'];

        if (empty($username) || empty($email) || empty($password) || empty($password_confirm)) {
            echo '<p style="color:red;">All fields are required.</p>';
            return;
        }

        if (!is_email($email)) {
            echo '<p style="color:red;">Please enter a valid email address.</p>';
            return;
        }

        if ($password !== $password_confirm) {
            echo '<p style="color:red;">Passwords do not match.</p>';
            return;
        }

        // Check if username or email already exists
        $user_exists = $wpdb->get_row($wpdb->prepare("SELECT id FROM $table_name WHERE username = %s OR email = %s", $username, $email));
        if ($user_exists) {
            echo '<p style="color:red;">Username or Email already exists. Please choose another.</p>';
            return;
        }

        $hashed_password = password_hash($password, PASSWORD_DEFAULT);

        $inserted = $wpdb->insert(
            $table_name,
            array(
                'username' => $username,
                'password' => $hashed_password,
                'email' => $email,
            ),
            array(
                '%s',
                '%s',
                '%s',
            )
        );

        if ($inserted) {
            echo '<p style="color:green;">Registration successful! You can now log in.</p>';
        } else {
            echo '<p style="color:red;">Registration failed. Please try again.</p>';
        }
    }
}
add_action('wp_loaded', 'cua_handle_registration'); // Use wp_loaded to ensure $wpdb is ready

/**
 * Shortcode for user login form.
 */
function cua_login_form_shortcode() {
    if (is_user_logged_in_cua()) {
        return '<p>You are already logged in as ' . esc_html(cua_get_current_user_username()) . '. <a href="' . wp_logout_url(home_url()) . '">Log out</a></p>';
    }

    ob_start();
    ?>
    <div id="cua-login-form">
        <h2>Login</h2>
        <form method="post" action="">
            <p>
                <label for="cua_login_username">Username or Email:</label><br/>
                <input type="text" id="cua_login_username" name="cua_login_username" required>
            </p>
            <p>
                <label for="cua_login_password">Password:</label><br/>
                <input type="password" id="cua_login_password" name="cua_login_password" required>
            </p>
            <p>
                <input type="submit" name="cua_login_submit" value="Login">
            </p>
            <?php wp_nonce_field('cua_login_action', 'cua_login_nonce'); ?>
        </form>
        <div id="cua-login-message"></div>
    </div>
    <?php
    return ob_get_clean();
}
add_shortcode('cua_login_form', 'cua_login_form_shortcode');

/**
 * Handle user login form submission.
 */
function cua_handle_login() {
    if (isset($_POST['cua_login_submit'])) {
        if (!isset($_POST['cua_login_nonce']) || !wp_verify_nonce($_POST['cua_login_nonce'], 'cua_login_action')) {
            wp_die('Security check failed.');
        }

        global $wpdb;
        $table_name = $wpdb->prefix . 'cua_users';

        $username_or_email = sanitize_text_field($_POST['cua_login_username']);
        $password = $_POST['cua_login_password'];

        if (empty($username_or_email) || empty($password)) {
            echo '<p style="color:red;">Both fields are required.</p>';
            return;
        }

        $user = $wpdb->get_row($wpdb->prepare(
            "SELECT id, username, password FROM $table_name WHERE username = %s OR email = %s",
            $username_or_email,
            $username_or_email
        ));

        if ($user && password_verify($password, $user->password)) {
            // Login successful
            cua_set_current_user($user->id, $user->username);
            echo '<p style="color:green;">Login successful! Redirecting...</p>';
            // Optional: Redirect user after successful login
            echo '<script type="text/javascript">window.location.href = "' . esc_url(home_url()) . '";</script>';
            exit;
        } else {
            echo '<p style="color:red;">Invalid username/email or password.</p>';
        }
    }
}
add_action('wp_loaded', 'cua_handle_login');

/**
 * Set current user in session.
 *
 * @param int    $user_id
 * @param string $username
 */
function cua_set_current_user($user_id, $username) {
    if (!session_id()) {
        session_start();
    }
    $_SESSION['cua_user_id'] = $user_id;
    $_SESSION['cua_username'] = $username;
}

/**
 * Check if a custom user is logged in.
 *
 * @return bool
 */
function is_user_logged_in_cua() {
    if (!session_id()) {
        session_start();
    }
    return isset($_SESSION['cua_user_id']) && isset($_SESSION['cua_username']);
}

/**
 * Get current logged-in custom user's ID.
 *
 * @return int|null
 */
function cua_get_current_user_id() {
    if (!session_id()) {
        session_start();
    }
    return isset($_SESSION['cua_user_id']) ? $_SESSION['cua_user_id'] : null;
}

/**
 * Get current logged-in custom user's username.
 *
 * @return string|null
 */
function cua_get_current_user_username() {
    if (!session_id()) {
        session_start();
    }
    return isset($_SESSION['cua_username']) ? $_SESSION['cua_username'] : null;
}

/**
 * WordPress Logout integration for custom users.
 * This function will log out the custom user when a regular WordPress logout link is clicked.
 */
function cua_logout_custom_user() {
    if (isset($_GET['action']) && $_GET['action'] == 'logout' && is_user_logged_in_cua()) {
        if (!session_id()) {
            session_start();
        }
        unset($_SESSION['cua_user_id']);
        unset($_SESSION['cua_username']);
        session_destroy();
    }
}
add_action('wp_logout', 'cua_logout_custom_user'); // Hook into WordPress logout action

/**
 * Start session on 'init' hook if not already started.
 * This is crucial for session-based authentication.
 */
function cua_start_session() {
    if (!session_id()) {
        session_start();
    }
}
add_action('init', 'cua_start_session');

/**
 * Shortcode for displaying custom user info and logout.
 */
function cua_user_info_shortcode() {
    if (is_user_logged_in_cua()) {
        $username = cua_get_current_user_username();
        $output = '<p>Welcome, ' . esc_html($username) . '!</p>';
        $output .= '<p><a href="' . wp_logout_url(home_url()) . '">Log out</a></p>';
        return $output;
    } else {
        return '<p>You are not logged in. <a href="' . esc_url(site_url('/login')) . '">Login</a> or <a href="' . esc_url(site_url('/register')) . '">Register</a>.</p>';
    }
}
add_shortcode('cua_user_info', 'cua_user_info_shortcode');


// Usage:
// 1. Activate the plugin.
// 2. Create a new page, e.g., "Register" and add the shortcode [cua_registration_form].
// 3. Create a new page, e.g., "Login" and add the shortcode [cua_login_form].
// 4. Create a new page, e.g., "User Dashboard" and add the shortcode [cua_user_info].
// 5. Navigate to these pages to test registration and login.
// 6. The plugin creates a custom table `wp_cua_users` (or `yourprefix_cua_users`).
